operating_state: Total revenues increased 41% to $65.1 billion driven by Google Search growth of 44% and YouTube ads growth of 43%. Google Cloud revenues grew 45% while Google Services operating income increased $9.5 billion to $24.0 billion as revenue growth outpaced expense increases. Google Cloud operating loss decreased $564 million from improved revenue scaling.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased to $27.6 billion with TAC rising $3.3 billion though TAC rate decreased to 21.6% from revenue mix shift toward Google properties. Other cost increases primarily from YouTube content acquisition and data center operations were partially offset by $591 million reduced depreciation from extended server useful life estimates. General and administrative expenses increased $500 million driven by legal matter charges and compensation expenses from 13% headcount growth.
material_events: Board authorized $50 billion share repurchase program with $12.6 billion repurchased during the quarter and $36.8 billion year-to-date. Completed Fitbit acquisition for $2.1 billion and exercised option to purchase St. John's Terminal for $2.1 billion. Supreme Court ruled in Google's favor on Oracle Java API case, concluding the litigation.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $671 million negative impact year-to-date with extensive hedging programs maintaining $16.1 billion in cash flow hedges and $36.8 billion in non-designated foreign exchange contracts. Effective tax rate increased to 17.9% from 15.8% due to higher pre-tax earnings in countries with higher statutory rates. Investment portfolio volatility contributed $5.7 billion increase in other income from unrealized gains.
forward_outlook: Management expects revenue growth rate effects from COVID-19 comparisons to decline through remainder of 2021 with depreciation expense reduction benefits from server useful life changes expected to diminish through year-end. Google Cloud remaining performance obligations of $37.1 billion with approximately half expected to be recognized over next 24 months. Continued investment focus on technical infrastructure and long-term growth initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing antitrust investigations including DOJ lawsuit on Search advertising, state AG complaints on advertising technology and Android operations, and EC formal investigation into advertising technology practices. European Commission fines totaling $9.5 billion remain under appeal with bank guarantees provided. Unrecognized tax benefits increased to $5.3 billion with transition tax of $5.6 billion payable through 2025.
